commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi 380 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi 380's sole 6G75 3.8L V6 petrol engine is generally robust when maintained, but timing belt neglect, oil leaks and idle control issues drive most UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt 6G75 engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded seals, bearings and timing components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Why 6G75 Engines Fail Catastrophically
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi 380 ES, LS, LX, SX, VRX, GT (2005-2008, 6G75, 6G75-1)
problems.rootCause: The 6G75 is an interference engine - if the timing belt snaps or jumps teeth, pistons strike valves causing bent valves, damaged pistons and potential head damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idlers, water pump; set timing marks
repairOptions.longevity: 60k mile interval; only viable before belt failure occurs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Restores like-new reliability; only option after catastrophic belt failure

problems.recommendation: On a Mitsubishi 380 worth £3,000-£8,000, preventative timing belt service at 60k miles is far cheaper than engine replacement. If the belt has already failed, a rebuilt 6G75 with upgraded components is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 6G75 engine prices for your Mitsubishi 380

problems.h4: Oil Leak - Rear Main Seal & Valve Cover Gasket Failure
problems.rootCause: Age-related hardening of rubber seals combined with heat cycling causes the rear main crank seal and valve cover gaskets to shrink and leak oil onto the gearbox or exhaust.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove gearbox or valve covers; replace rear main seal or valve cover gaskets with updated materials
repairOptions.longevity: 30-50k miles if other seals are also aged; may recur if block has wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with all new seals, gaskets and upgraded materials, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive solution; eliminates all seal-related leaks with warranty protection

problems.recommendation: For isolated oil leaks on a otherwise healthy 6G75, seal replacement is cost-effective. If multiple leaks exist or the engine has high mileage, a rebuilt unit with all-new seals offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi 380 6G75 engine with new seals

problems.h4: Idle Stalling - IAC Valve & Sensor Failure
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup on the Idle Air Control valve or failing throttle position sensors cause erratic idle, stalling at lights, or rough cold starts - common on high-mileage grey imports.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean or replace IAC valve, throttle body service, sensor diagnostics
repairOptions.longevity: 20-40k miles; may recur if underlying carbon buildup not addressed

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full OBD2 scan, replace TPS/MAF sensors, ECU adaptation reset
repairOptions.longevity: 30-60k miles; resolves most stalling issues without engine work

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new sensors, ECU compatibility check, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only necessary if stalling is symptom of deeper internal wear or compression loss

problems.recommendation: Start with IAC valve cleaning and sensor diagnostics - most 380 stalling issues are resolved without engine replacement. Only consider a rebuilt engine if compression tests show internal wear.
problems.cta: Compare Mitsubishi 380 engine diagnostic and replacement options

problems.h4: Excessive Oil Consumption - Piston Ring Wear
problems.rootCause: Worn piston rings or cylinder bore scoring allows oil to enter combustion chambers, causing blue exhaust smoke, fouled spark plugs and increased oil top-up frequency.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace valve stem seals, PCV system service, compression test
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; does not address worn rings or bore scoring

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new pistons, rings, honed bores, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only permanent solution for genuine ring wear; restores oil consumption to factory specs

problems.recommendation: Confirm oil consumption with a compression test first. If rings are worn, a rebuilt 6G75 is more cost-effective than repeated top-end repairs on a 15+ year old engine.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt Mitsubishi 380 6G75 engine with new rings

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi 380
finalCta.paragraph: Grey import Mitsubishi 380 owners face unique challenges finding reliable engine replacements. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who understand the 6G75 V6, offer transparent pricing, and supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components that address known failure points. With nationwide delivery, supply & fit options, and a minimum 12-month warranty on all rebuilt units, you can get your Australian-spec 380 back on UK roads without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi 380 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi 380 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi 380 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi 380's 2005-2008 production timeline, organised so grey import owners can quickly identify their 6G75 engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Mitsubishi 380 launched in Australia with the DB Series I, introducing the 6G75 3.8L V6 as the sole engine option for all UK grey imports.
keyChanges: Mitsubishi 380 replaces Magna/Verada range in Australian market
New DB platform with updated suspension and interior
6G75 SOHC 24-valve V6 introduced as only engine option
Automatic and manual transmission options from launch
knownFor: Solid build quality from Australian manufacturing
Early models may have sensor calibration differences vs later series
Timing belt service critical at 60k miles to prevent catastrophic failure
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005 Mitsubishi 380 replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi 380 ES engine replacement • 6G75 timing belt failure cost • 380 LS oil leak repair

years.description: Series II brought minor ECU and sensor refinements while retaining the core 6G75 architecture across all 380 variants.
keyChanges: Updated engine management calibration for emissions compliance
Revised sensor packages for improved diagnostics
SX and LX variants gain additional equipment levels
Minor interior and exterior styling refresh
knownFor: Improved cold-start behaviour with updated ECU mapping
Compatibility with Series I engines with minor bracket differences
IAC valve stalling reports increase on higher-mileage examples
years.ticker: Mitsubishi 380 SX engine supply • 6G75 IAC valve replacement • 380 Series II compatibility check

years.description: The final Series III models feature the most refined 6G75 calibration and updated sensor packages, representing the peak of 380 development.
keyChanges: Final ECU software update with improved diagnostics
Updated oxygen sensors and emissions equipment
VRX and GT performance trims gain sport suspension and styling
mainEngines: 6G75-2 - 3.8L petrol/LPG (fleet-spec Australian imports, rare in UK)
knownFor: Most desirable grey import 380s due to latest specifications
VRX models command premium in UK used market
Timing belt and oil leak issues persist if maintenance neglected

years.ticker: Mitsubishi 380 VRX engine replacement • 6G75-2 LPG compatibility • 380 Series III ECU coding

Minor sensor and ECU updates require compatibility checks
Transmission bellhousing differs between auto/manual variants
knownFor: Universal engine compatibility simplifies grey import replacement sourcing
Always verify transmission type and ECU coding when ordering

faq.h2: Mitsubishi 380 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi 380 engine replacement cost?
items.answer: Used Mitsubishi 380 6G75 engines start from £1,200, while professionally reconditioned units range from £2,100-£3,200 supply-only. Get three quotes to compare prices from UK specialists.
keyPoints: Prices vary by engine condition, mileage and warranty length
Supply & fit service adds £800-£1,500 depending on location
All rebuilt engines include upgraded timing components and seals
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi 380 engine prices from verified UK suppliers

items.question: What engine code is in the Mitsubishi 380?
items.answer: All Mitsubishi 380 models use the 6G75 3.8L SOHC 24-valve V6 petrol engine, with sub-codes 6G75-1 (standard) or 6G75-2 (factory LPG) depending on original specification.
keyPoints: 6G75 is the only engine ever fitted to the 380 range
Sub-code determines transmission bellhousing and fuel system
Enter your VIN to confirm exact 6G75 variant for your vehicle
items.cta: Verify your Mitsubishi 380 6G75 engine code instantly

items.question: Is the Mitsubishi 380 engine an interference engine?
items.answer: Yes, the 6G75 V6 in the Mitsubishi 380 is an interference engine, meaning timing belt failure will cause pistons to strike valves, resulting in catastrophic engine damage.
keyPoints: Timing belt must be replaced every 60,000 miles preventatively
Belt failure typically bends valves and damages pistons/cylinder head
Rebuilt engine replacement is the only reliable fix after belt failure
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 6G75 engine after timing belt failure

items.question: How long does a Mitsubishi 380 engine last?
items.answer: Well-maintained 6G75 engines can exceed 150,000 miles, but timing belt adherence at 60k-mile intervals is critical to avoid catastrophic interference damage on this V6.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes with 5W-30 fully synthetic extend engine life
Address oil leaks early to prevent secondary damage
High-mileage examples benefit from rebuilt units with upgraded components
items.cta: Compare long-life rebuilt Mitsubishi 380 engine options

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Mitsubishi 380?
items.answer: The 6G75 is the only engine designed for the Mitsubishi 380 chassis; alternative swaps require extensive fabrication and are not recommended for grey import UK vehicles.
keyPoints: 6G75 mounts, ECU and wiring are model-specific to the 380
Cross-compatibility with other Mitsubishi V6 engines is limited
Stick with 6G75 replacement for reliable, warranty-backed results
items.cta: Find guaranteed-fit 6G75 engines for your Mitsubishi 380

items.question: Do I need to replace the gearbox when replacing my 380 engine?
items.answer: No, the engine and gearbox are separate components on the Mitsubishi 380; specify automatic or manual transmission when ordering your 6G75 replacement to ensure bellhousing compatibility.
keyPoints: Automatic and manual 6G75 engines have different bellhousing patterns
Your existing gearbox can be reused if in good condition
Clutch or torque converter service recommended during engine replacement
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi 380 auto and manual 6G75 engine prices

items.question: Will a Mitsubishi 380 engine fit my right-hand drive UK car?
items.answer: Yes, all Mitsubishi 380 engines come from Australian right-hand drive models, making them directly compatible with UK grey import 380s without modification.
keyPoints: Australian-spec 6G75 engines match UK RHD 380 mounting points
ECU coding may require specialist adaptation for UK registration
Full import documentation provided with all grey import engines
items.cta: Get compliant 6G75 engines for your UK-registered Mitsubishi 380

items.question: What warranty comes with a replacement Mitsubishi 380 engine?
items.answer: Minimum 6-month warranty on used 6G75 engines, 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on reconditioned and rebuilt units, with terms varying by supplier in our UK network.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Rebuilt engines include upgraded components addressing known failures
Always confirm exact warranty terms with your chosen supplier before purchase
items.cta: Compare warranty terms on Mitsubishi 380 engine replacements

items.question: How do I know which Mitsubishi 380 engine I need?
items.answer: Use our VIN decoder or registration lookup to confirm your exact 6G75 sub-code, transmission type and model year for guaranteed compatibility with your grey import 380.
keyPoints: Engine code is stamped on the block near the oil filter housing
Transmission type (auto/manual) determines bellhousing specification
Series I/II/III sensors may require ECU compatibility checks
items.cta: Identify your exact Mitsubishi 380 6G75 engine code now

items.question: Can I get supply and fit for my Mitsubishi 380 engine?
items.answer: Yes, our nationwide network of UK specialists offers supply & fit service for Mitsubishi 380 6G75 engines, including installation, ECU coding and post-fit testing with warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Supply & fit adds £800-£1,500 to engine price depending on location
Garages experienced with Australian-spec grey imports preferred
Single point of contact simplifies the replacement process
